title: M4 Heat-Sink Solver-Backed D1 Transfer Gate Results
description: This report summarizes geometry-varied solver-backed heat-sink D1 label-scarcity transfer runs comparing scratch, Thermal GeoPT pretraining, and the original GeoPT checkpoint.
interpretation_rule: A positive M4 signal means a pretrained group improves scratch by about 10% relative L2 at 25 or 50 labels, or reaches similar error with fewer labels, without degrading max-temperature or hotspot metrics. Original GeoPT is a control for general GeoPT geometry-boundary priors; Thermal GeoPT must beat or complement it to support a thermal-specific pretraining claim.
